req.title: Announcement rollout — variant 4
req.statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates: (1) a system-admin can publish an org-wide announcement targeting one or more departments; (2) submission with no department selected must be rejected with HTTP 422; (3) publishing is rate-limited per user. Four test areas: EP — happy-path publish with valid departments; NEG — publish with zero departments selected yields 422; BVA — exactly one department selected (lower boundary of valid selection); STC — rate-limit state transition from allowed to throttled. The violation in the original response was test[3] having kind=negative with heuristic=STC. STC (State Transition Coverage) describes a state-machine traversal heuristic and is not inherently negative or positive — however the rule checker flags the combination. The fix is to change the kind of the rate-limit test to 'positive' since we are exercising a valid state transition path (allowed → throttled is the expected, designed system behaviour, not an error input path); the NEG heuristic is reserved for invalid-input tests. This preserves all four tests while satisfying the constraint that STC tests must not carry kind=negative.

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_with_valid_departments
preconditions: At least two departments exist in the organisation
No duplicate announcement with same title exists
actions: Fill in announcement title and body
Select two or more departments from the department picker
expected: HTTP 201 or redirect to announcement detail page
Announcement record appears in the database with correct department associations
Target employees receive a notification event
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_publish_announcement_with_valid_departments(admin_session, page, db_announcement_query, notification_bus):
    page.goto("/org-announcements/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-title']", "Q3 All-Hands")
    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-body']", "Join us for the Q3 update.")
    <select_departments>(page, dept_ids=["dept-101", "dept-202"])
    page.click("[data-testid='publish-btn']")
    page.wait_for_url("**/org-announcements/**")
    assert "/org-announcements/" in page.url
    record = db_announcement_query(title="Q3 All-Hands")
    assert record is not None
    assert set(record["department_ids"]) == {"dept-101", "dept-202"}
    events = notification_bus.get_events(topic="announcement.published", announcement_id=record["id"])
    assert len(events) >= 1

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_without_departments_rejected
actions: Fill in announcement title and body
expected: Form submission is rejected with a 422 Unprocessable Entity response
An inline validation error message is displayed referencing department selection
No announcement record is created in the database
trace: R-WEB-090 — error pathway: 422 when no departments selected
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_publish_announcement_without_departments_rejected(admin_session, page, api_client, db_announcement_query):
    page.goto("/org-announcements/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-title']", "No-Dept Announcement")
    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-body']", "This should fail.")
    # Intentionally skip department selection
    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST") as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='publish-btn']")
    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status == 422
    error_msg = page.locator("[data-testid='dept-error']").inner_text()
    assert "department" in error_msg.lower()
    record = db_announcement_query(title="No-Dept Announcement")
    assert record is None

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_with_exactly_one_department
preconditions: At least one department exists in the organisation
actions: Fill in announcement title and body
Select exactly one department (lower boundary of valid selection)
expected: Announcement is created successfully (HTTP 201 or success redirect)
Announcement is associated with exactly the one selected department
Employees in that department receive a notification
trace: R-WEB-090 — boundary: minimum valid department count = 1
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_publish_announcement_with_exactly_one_department(admin_session, page, db_announcement_query, notification_bus):
    page.goto("/org-announcements/new")
    title = "Single-Dept Boundary Test"
    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-title']", title)
    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-body']", "Boundary check body.")
    <select_departments>(page, dept_ids=["dept-101"])  # exactly one
    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST") as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='publish-btn']")
    assert resp_info.value.status in (200, 201, 302)
    record = db_announcement_query(title=title)
    assert record is not None
    assert record["department_ids"] == ["dept-101"]
    events = notification_bus.get_events(topic="announcement.published", announcement_id=record["id"])
    assert len(events) >= 1

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_rate_limit_enforced
preconditions: Rate-limit counter for this admin user is at the allowed threshold (N publications within the window)
actions: Submit N valid announcements (each with at least one department) to exhaust the rate limit
Submit one additional announcement beyond the limit
expected: First N submissions succeed with HTTP 201
The (N+1)th submission is rejected with HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
No additional announcement record is created for the rejected request
tests.executable:
import pytest

RATE_LIMIT = <get_rate_limit_config>("org_announcements_per_window")

def test_publish_announcement_rate_limit_enforced(admin_api_client, db_announcement_query, reset_rate_limit):
    reset_rate_limit(user_id="admin-001", endpoint="org-announcements")
    payload = {"title": "RL Test", "body": "body", "department_ids": ["dept-101"]}
    for i in range(RATE_LIMIT):
        payload["title"] = f"RL Test {i}"
        resp = admin_api_client.post("/org-announcements", json=payload)
        assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 on request {i}, got {resp.status_code}"
    payload["title"] = "RL Test OVER"
    over_resp = admin_api_client.post("/org-announcements", json=payload)
    assert over_resp.status_code == 429
    record = db_announcement_query(title="RL Test OVER")
    assert record is None
